title: lycopodium selago var. haleakalae
intro: Lycopodium selago var. haleakalae, also known as Haleakala Club Moss, is a rare and endemic plant species found in Hawaii. This plant is a type of lycopod, a group of ancient plants that reproduce via spores, and is used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant qualities. The health benefits of Lycopodium selago var. haleakalae include relief from respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as skin conditions like eczema and acne. In herbal preparations, the plant's spores are typically dried and used to make teas, tinctures, and poultices, which can be used to treat a range of ailments.
